title: Notion vs Linear
description: All-in-one workspace vs focused issue tracking. We compare features, pricing, and use cases.
verdict: Notion combines docs, wikis, databases, and project management into a flexible all-in-one workspace that adapts to how any team organizes information. Linear delivers a focused, keyboard-first issue tracking experience with sprint planning, velocity metrics, and roadmap views that engineering teams love. Teams that need lightweight project management alongside rich documentation and database capabilities will prefer Notion's versatility. Engineering teams focused on fast-paced issue tracking and agile development cycles will appreciate Linear's speed and precision for their daily workflows.

faqs.question: Which is better, Notion or Linear?
faqs.answer: It depends on your needs. Notion is better for general project management, documentation, and all-in-one workspace use cases. Linear is better for teams focused primarily on issue tracking, sprint planning, and engineering workflows.

faqs.question: Can Notion and Linear be used together?
faqs.answer: Yes, many teams use Notion for documentation and wikis while using Linear for day-to-day issue tracking and sprint management. Both have APIs and integrations that make this workflow possible.

faqs.question: Is Notion good for software development teams?
faqs.answer: Notion works well for software teams that need documentation, roadmaps, and lightweight task tracking. However, teams needing advanced agile features like velocity tracking and cycle metrics may prefer Linear's purpose-built approach.

faqs.question: Does Linear have a free tier?
faqs.answer: Yes, Linear offers a free tier for teams of up to 10 users. Notion's free plan is more generous with unlimited blocks and up to 10 collaborators, plus a 7-day page history.
